hook.text: The alcohol industry spends billions marketing fun, success, and romance—while 3 million people die from its products.

buildup.text: Sports sponsorships, social media campaigns, and celebrity endorsements normalize heavy drinking—especially among youth.

discovery.text: Exposure to alcohol advertising increases the likelihood that young people will start drinking and drink heavily.

twist.text: The industry promotes 'responsible drinking' campaigns—but research shows these protect brand image, not reduce harm.

climax.text: Comprehensive bans on alcohol advertising—like those for tobacco—reduce consumption and are recommended by the WHO.

punchline.text: An industry that profits from addiction will never honestly promote moderation.

quiz.question: What effect does alcohol advertising have on young people?
options: It increases the likelihood they will start drinking
It reduces drinking by raising awareness
